channel.title: Comments for page "Free Consultation"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Free Consultation"

item.title: Headcanon
item.content:encoded: My headcanon of dado came from this article and it is that he is a non human enity existing outside of our universe and knows exactly 100% whats going on but just does that stuff to be silly because he is immortal and likes fucking with humans. I think the memetic phrase "u trust dado" or "trust dado" is infectoius and can influence people, that is why people buy his stuff. I am pretty sure that a lot of people on earth are infected with the "u trust dado" memetic but no one realizes it including the SCPF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is an ominous aftertaste to dado's last line that I quite like.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was certainly the fastest, most effective shift from funny to scary in an article.

item.title: solution to dado dilemma
item.content:encoded:
no, see, is dado, and can prove it

in france fry and borger, dado is worry of birds, right. here, birds is why dado becomes shado dado. merchant carton drink is giving big capitalism opportunity here to dado, who is fine capitalist. but capitalist instincts force bird on dado when in danger of lose valuable deal. dado keyboard make funny typings as dado is succumb to his duck. his mallard force him to fix capitals and get dark lady name right so have potential customer. dado is genius capitalist who have robert mitchell give him goose when business partners need dado to be professional. that is the answer, it is dado with his ingenious geese.

Or rather, his geas. No, I'm not apologizing. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this interpretation of Dado more than the "normal" Dado.

item.title: It happened
item.content:encoded:
I've been reading this and I know a thing for sure…

You made dado narratively interesting.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I appreciate the alternate character interpretation here. I wouldn't consider this mainline dado, but it's a neat little addition to the dado multiverse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus that final line. Chills.

dado rules be damned, this is fucking awesome. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, thanks.

Me using Chrome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Raised eyebrow emoji. What browser are you using?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this, but what is that last symbol? Neither my computer or my phone displays it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
im shakingh and cryinhg rn dado would never do this guys i farded

In all seriousness, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell, I can get behind this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe I know the dread identity of the final text's poster:

Allow me to explain: Taking this and strange man on phone interrupt hamster holiday, a dark picture emerges. Relevant madness and quotes shortened for your convenience

The Evidence
This is all headcanon. Also madness.

dado's keyboard was broken by Robert Mitchell…

in fact one day robert mitchell is do the walk about and suddenly uppercase key is broke.

But he has the ability to bring it back…

OH NO HAMSTER WHAT HAVE U DONE. CAPITALIZATION KEY FIX BUT NOW ALL KEY ARE CAPITALIZATION

And Robert 'playing' on the keyboard was what got around the original block…

sry we get disconnect. hamster like to play on keyboard.


This all points to the true power behind the throne.
Dread headcanon lies within...
hamster

None other than Robert Mitchell, the guinea pig himself

And in just a little over a week too. That's not too bad, especially for a non-SCP.

this is why u trust dado

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i dont like that last message

if i had to guess id say this comes across as somebody with a great deal of power pretending to be dado long enough to compromise iris dark via scattershot memetics

like, dado has rules dado must follow imo (definitely from an external context, possibly from an internal one as well)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never really liked dado, he just seemed annoying to me. But this, this made me like dado. This made him seem much less like a fad or a joke and more like an interesting character.
+1

item.title: Good dado tale
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for not making dado a laughing stock. People only treat him like a meme, but this character can be much more.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YES! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know who this dado guy is but he looks like he's made of CHEESE hahahaha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do both…

item.title: Re: No canon, right?
item.content:encoded: Ha, that's a great retort! I admit, your logic is flawless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was the first article in a while where I've actually enjoyed dado, and it was that last line that did it for me - in four words it turned him into an actual character with depth. He's not just lolfoundation anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We are in concordance.

item.title: Re: No canon, right?
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I don't actually mind. I've written many pieces that were so benign they got few if any comments. I'd much rather receive a bunch of comments, even if some of those are questioning whether or not a character's actions make sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A valid point.

You all have offered your critique, I've offered my retort, and I don't think I have anything further to add. Readers can either accept my justifications as adequate or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm into this.

item.title: New Headcanon
item.content:encoded:
People here are right, dado capslock key broken.

Whoever said this was actually dado?

item.title: Re: No canon, right?
item.content:encoded: "There's no canon" doesn't mean that articles are immune to criticism in their characterization. You have every right to portray whichever character in whatever way you see fit, but people would overlay their own headcanon over your article and criticize how far it deviates — and those headcanons are as valid as your own interpretation even though you're the author, because there is no canon.

item.title: No canon, right?
item.content:encoded:
The rules seem pretty straightforward: upvoted things can stay as they are unless they violate site rules; tales and SCP entries can contradict each other.

So why the fuss from a few people? Couldn't I make an entry about dado being Jack Bright in a mosquito's body? Couldn't I make an entry where all of the GOI's enter teams into a combination baking/dancing reality TV show?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really, really don't agree with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So far the only 3 people who have complained about Iris being too chatty have been Site leets, and to be frank I think you guys might be reading a bit too much into this. This is supposed to be a light-hearted, silly piece, and Iris needs to talk for it to work. If she just ignored dado, there'd be no tale. I think I've justified enough why she does that, and I'm sorry if that doesn't fit with your view of the character or how she would act in this situation.

This is an impotent response to legitimate critique and frankly it veers closer to an ad hominem. This doesn't work as a light-hearted or silly piece, especially when the wham line breaks character to the point of ominousness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For my part I'm happy to let Iris' willingness to continue the conversation be explained by nothing more than sheer curiosity at how dado is managing the impossible in getting the messages to appear at all.

If someone texted me inside a Faraday cage, you bet I'm reading that, because that's definitely a text from someone capable of violating even basic physics. Maybe Iris should be more inured to the miraculous than I would be, given her position, but I still feel sheer curiosity is enough justification on its own here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Messages embedded with memetic hazards, which he proves he can send even after she switches sim cards, proving he can send them no matter what device she's using. Plus he has hacked her phone, so he can potentially do a lot more than just send messages. Iris needs to use a phone, so she has to deal with this.

Sure, and the explanation that she's trying to trace his IP address (even though they're texting? I think I understand what you mean here though) fits in with that, so I'll at least accept that, but I think then it seems like Iris going about it the way she's doing doesn't really feel like it works.

Yeah, and the President of the United States wouldn't use an off the shelf iPhone and leave it lying around in a golf cart. Wait…

Yes, this is one reason why reading about leaders of organizations dealing with the anomalous being comically incompetent isn't entertaining or quirky. Another reason is because there's so many tales of leaders of organizations dealing with the anomalous being comically incompetent on the site that it still feels worn-out. And yes, Iris doesn't go all that way here, because it's a (small-scale) thing, but I don't think it works here or is effective here anyway.

I don't see why this is a big deal.

If Iris is keeping dado talking to trace where he is, who's to say dado isn't doing the same on his end?

So far the only 3 people who have complained about Iris being too chatty have been Site leets

To address this first; I don't think me being on the site for a long time has any bearings on my taste in silliness, humor, light-heartedness, or judging works for being successful in any of those areas. I think this tale sort of works in that, and I already mentioned that I like the concept (which is inherently a silly concept, so of course I'm fine with the silliness). There were certain parts of the tale I think didn't work, so I left my views and critiques on it.

and to be frank I think you guys might be reading a bit too much into this.

People shouldn't give silly pieces a free pass just because they are silly, or light-hearted. So when I read something like that, I judge it the same way I do anything else. I didn't read too much into it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I fucking loved this.

This kind of makes me feel like a dingus because I was tossing around something like it in my head but you executed it in a vastly superior fashion. The way you turn Daddy into a character, you don't brush away all his ridiculous shit, but you embrace it; regardless of how nonsensical or silly it may seem to us, Dark understands that, at his level of expertise, it means something to him. Even if he's just a crackpot weirdo living out of a basement, or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's still delusional weird shit but Dark has been infected and it makes sense to her.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
She needs to find out how he got past her block because as far as she knows that shouldn't be possible. He proves himself at least a minor security threat by doing that, and it wouldn't do to ignore it.

If it makes everyone feel better, let's say that she was trying to trace his IP address or something while they were chatting.

all dado would do here if she didn't keep talking to him is… spam her phone with messages

Messages embedded with memetic hazards, which he proves he can send even after she switches sim cards, proving he can send them no matter what device she's using. Plus he has hacked her phone, so he can potentially do a lot more than just send messages. Iris needs to use a phone, so she has to deal with this.

An O5 wouldn't do that, nor Al Fine.

Yeah, and the President of the United States wouldn't use an off the shelf iPhone and leave it lying around in a golf cart. Wait…

As for the Edit, please refer to my comment on Souless's post. But to summarize, I really don't think she gives out that much information, nor is the information all that compromising. What she says amounts to 'her phone runs on a proprietary OS and software that should be unhackable, she has anti-malware that can detect memetics and that she couldn't get rid of him even after switching sim cards or going into a Faraday room' none of which is very compromising and all of which dado would have or could have known or inferred from his end. I don't see why this is a big deal.

So far the only 3 people who have complained about Iris being too chatty have been Site leets, and to be frank I think you guys might be reading a bit too much into this. This is supposed to be a light-hearted, silly piece, and Iris needs to talk for it to work. If she just ignored dado, there'd be no tale. I think I've justified enough why she does that, and I'm sorry if that doesn't fit with your view of the character or how she would act in this situation.

Iris was caught off guard by someone successfully hacking her phone, and because it was dado she underestimated him and demanded an explanation and ordered him to stop. In hindsight, this was not the best course of action, and when she realizes that, it's too late to just ignore him so she tries to defuse the situation. That's what happened, it's canon, let's move on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was an eyebrow raised emoji.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was being facetious. dado didn't get his powers from a radioactive pill bug. Probably.

…

Maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Everyone is talking about dado's last message, but I had a question about Iris's. Is it an emoji? Which one? It's just showing as a rectangle for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Totally agreed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this got me addicted to dado. upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was my intro to dado. I now love dado. dado is now one of my SCP favorites. dado is all. Thank you, Dr. Chandra.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m guessing it’s mostky out of curiosity. She’s wondering what potential he has.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t know who/what dado is but this is beautiful

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept of this enough that I won't downvote right away, but why would Dark continue to talk to him after the first time he's found a way to get around her blocklist? So far as I can see, all dado would do here if she didn't keep talking to him is… spam her phone with messages. Which seems fine, considering she's a literal head of an anomalous organization and doesn't need to address anyone she isn't trustful of who's messaging her phone. I get you needed the setup for this, but I cannot understand why this would happen. An O5 wouldn't do that, nor Al Fine.

EDIT: Also, why is she giving out so much information to dado? I assumed the intent behind all of what I'm saying is "She doesn't see him as a threat", then why is she acting so rattled? If she's trying to get dado to spill information, then why is the way she's engaging with him seeming like that's not her intention? If she's just chatty, she's not acting smart, and it doesn't feel in-line with any other Dark I've seen on the site, even Iris.

EDIT 2: FTR, I'll just say I like dado's final line and think it's a good way to end the tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to think it's true but it gave him horrible brain-altering chemicals along with reality manipulation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last line brought dado from Patrick Star to Anton Chigurh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you, Ms. Dark.

I was getting lunch before I sat down and read this, and by coincidence I thought how it amused me that Dado never seems to be aware of their own effects, or at least not fully.
This last line from them though, is exactly along the lines of what was my following thought:
"What if they are aware? VERY aware?"

Silly Dado is, well, silly!

Serious Dado makes my blood run cold.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just assumed the origin story about being a humble clerk at Walgreens who was bitten by a pill bug was a lie. Then again, in his own eccentric way, dado has always been a very forthright person. And he does badly want Iris's help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
dado borger and france fry closed because of the not seagulls eating all the france frys

I swear to god you wrote this line to appeal directly to me.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's something that really only works if you're familiar with other articles featuring the character, but who would've thought proper SPaG could lead to a wham line?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Understandable, and in all fairness to you this may just be somewhat clunky exposition on my part, however I would like to point out that 'all her secrets' is an overstatement. She just says 'I programmed this myself using Chaos Runes' and that her phone is flagging his texts as memetic malware, which isn't all that much, and since dado has already hacked her phone he knows at least that much already. She doesn't say anything compromising that he doesn't already know.

And at that point, she's not so much disturbed as irritated. She's still underestimating dado, and does not regard it as 'crisis situation', just a nuisance. She's not rattled until the text where she says she's rattled. She's competent, but not infallible, and here at least dado is in the incarnation of obfusticating stupidity.

Edit: Come to think of it, dado gives away his origin story without cause, and that's much more compromising. Foolish, foolish dado.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That line sold the whole piece for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Thank you, Ms. Dark."
You broke me
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That last text was truly unsettling.

Yeah, agreed. That pushed me to an upvote, personally. Though I do agree that Iris did give away more information than made sense.

That said, the "will martian, cottage and grimdark buy from new dado business?" line made me need to look away from the screen a bit. I just. Could not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It takes a lot to rattle Dark. dado is a lot. I'm imagining a collaboration between dado and Totleigh Software, because I really didn't need to sleep tonight.

Yes, dado, focus on pharma.

That last text was truly unsettling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be fair, it is quite common for a brit. At least, it is 'round 'ere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I downvoted for this reason. There's no reason why she would reveal her own information about what she is capable of doing to dado merely because she is disturbed. In fact, her disturbance should IMO engage her with what she knows to do in crisis situations such as this. That she instead informs him all of her secrets like a guilty teenager completely broke my suspension of disbelief that she's supposed to be competent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I noticed this as well. While I'm not familiar with the character, so it may be justified, it reads to me like a verbal conversation, rather than a text message - the grammar and spelling is perfect, but with the casual tone that suggests someone is speaking. There are two types of people - those who swear in text and those who spell text messages correctly - but doing both at the same time is not very common.
